title: Competitive level samples
description: Coach's rating of a team's skill/intensity level. Consumed by the SDUI create-team wizard's competitive_level select field. Not tied to age or sport — a `beginner` under-8 team is different from a `beginner` senior team, but the level captures the internal coach's rating.

levels.description: Casual participation. No formal competition. Members join for enjoyment, fitness, or social connection. Sessions focus on fun, not skill progression.
levels.example_context: Adult Sunday football pickup, gym HIIT class, community yoga.

levels.description: New to the sport / activity. Focus on fundamental skills + rules + safe technique. Progression is measured, not fast.
levels.example_context: Intro-to-tennis class for adults, kids' first swimming lessons, Learn-to-Skate.

levels.description: Comfortable with basics. Building tactical + technical depth. May play in local leagues at a low tier.
levels.example_context: Junior club team, adult local-league team, high-school JV squad.

levels.description: Serious about improvement. Plays in competitive leagues, prepares for tournaments. Structured training + video review + fitness testing.
levels.example_context: Academy senior squad, high-school varsity, regional-league team, elite age-group swim squad.

levels.description: Top of local/regional pyramid. National-league or national-team pathway. Dedicated strength + conditioning + performance analysis + nutrition support.
levels.typical_frequency: 5-6 sessions per week + weekend match + tournaments
levels.example_context: National academy squad, professional-club under-18, elite performance program.

levels.description: Explicitly organised for competition entry — tournaments, championships, invitational events. Distinct roster + training block per competition cycle. May overlap with elite when the competition team is composed of an academy's top performers.
levels.typical_frequency: 5-7 sessions per week during competition cycle; 3-4 out of season
levels.example_context: Regional championship squad, national tournament team, elite competition group.

notes: The `competitive_level` field is nullable — teams may skip it when the tenant's shape doesn't warrant it (a gym class is recreational by default; explicit setting is optional).
The catalogue is descriptive, not prescriptive. Tenants may use levels differently across their teams — the audit trail captures the intent.
